description_long: This object is a flintlock pistol, characterized by its long, slender barrel and a curved wooden grip. The body is primarily composed of polished wood with visible grain patterns, complemented by metallic components including the barrel, trigger, and lock mechanism. The lock features a prominent hammer and frizzen, typical of early firearm designs. The trigger guard and barrel bands are metallic, providing structural reinforcement and aesthetic contrast to the wood. The muzzle is simple and unadorned, with no visible modern attachments. The overall design is reminiscent of historical firearms used in the 18th and early 19th centuries.
description: A flintlock pistol with a curved wooden grip, metallic barrel, and classic lock mechanism, featuring polished wood and metal details.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the pistol, with the curved wooden grip, metallic barrel, and detailed lock mechanism visible.
description_view_1: Front view down the barrel, highlighting the circular muzzle and the alignment of the barrel with the lock mechanism barely visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wooden grip and metallic barrel, with the lock mechanism partially visible from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the grip and the top of the barrel, emphasizing the curvature of the handle and the alignment of the barrel.
